name: Terrace Hills Golf Course
about: Terrace Hills Golf Course is an 18-hole championship course located in Altoona, Iowa, offering a balance of challenge and playability for golfers of all skill levels. The facility includes practice facilities, a performance studio with simulator bays, professional instruction, and club repair and fitting services. Beyond the main course, the venue features a mini-golf course, clubhouse for gatherings, and options for leagues, lessons, and events.
why_golfers_like_it: Comprehensive practice facilities and a dedicated performance studio for skill improvement
Variety of amenities including mini-golf, clubhouse, and simulator bays for diverse experiences
Professional instruction available for all ages and skill levels with personalized lessons

food_and_drink.notes: Restaurant likely serves alcohol; no specific BYOB policy mentioned

simulator_hardware.notes: Simulator bays available but specific hardware not specified

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: The venue offers club repair and fitting services, but it's recommended to bring your own clubs for the best experience. Club rentals may be available but not explicitly mentioned.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: The venue has an on-site restaurant, and there's no mention of a BYOB policy. It's likely best to dine at the restaurant or inquire directly about outside food and drink policies.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The time for 18 holes isn't specified, but the venue offers 9-hole leagues with specific formats. Typically, 18 holes can take 4-5 hours depending on pace of play.

faq.question: Is the facility kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the venue is very kid-friendly, featuring a dedicated mini-golf course, junior golf camps, and PGA Jr. League programs specifically designed for young golfers.
